behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific implementation details not mentioned in instruction.md: test_rope_theta_patched checks that __init__ sets hf_config.rope_theta with default 1000000; test_shared_mapping_dynamic_layers checks _weight_to_hf_format returns exactly 2 entries with specific name patterns like 'model.layers.{n}'; test_safetensor_io checks _get_safetensor_io returns SafeTensorIO class by name; test_convert_mtp_param checks _convert_mtp_param method with exact parameter name mappings like 'mtp.layer

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what's broken (rope_theta lookup, wrong layer index, wrong I/O class, hardcoded mappings, incomplete MTP conversion) but does not specify exact code changes, line numbers, or the specific implementation approach to fix them.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on specific method names like _convert_mtp_param, _get_safetensor_io, _weight_to_hf_format, and _SHARED_STATE_DICT_MAPPING. They also check exact return structures (e.g., test_shared_mapping_dynamic_layers expects exactly 2 names/tensors, test_convert_mtp_param checks exact output name patterns like 'model.layers.{n}.shared_head.norm.weight'). An alternative correct fix using different internal method names or structures would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use _run_python_test to execute actual Python code via subprocess.run(), importing and calling GLM4MoELiteBridge methods, creating instances, and verifying behavioral outputs. They are not text-matching tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests exercise real behavioral changes: test_rope_theta_patched verifies rope_theta is extracted from rope_parameters dict (the stub class doesn't do this); test_convert_mtp_param verifies _convert_mtp_param exists and returns correct mappings (the stub has no such method); test_safetensor_io verifies the correct IO class is returned (the stub inherits the wrong FP8 one). A stub/pass implementation would not satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five behavioral issues described in instruction.md (rope_theta lookup, MTP weight name conversion, safetensor I/O class, shared weight mapping, MTP parameter name conversion) have corresponding fail-to-pass tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear sections (gates/static, pass-to-pass/repo_tests, fail-to-pass/pr_diff), descriptive function names, and detailed docstrings explaining what each test verifies and why it would fail on the base commit.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code modification, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: File paths, class names, method names, and variable names in the instruction and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md names the exact file (slime_plugins/mbridge/glm4moe_lite.py), the exact class (GLM4MoELiteBridge), specific parent methods (_build_config, _get_safetensor_io), specific internal attributes (_SHARED_STATE_DICT_MAPPING), and specific implementation details (layer 61 hardcoded, rope_parameters dict structure). This significantly reduces the localization challenge.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config-edit task.
